steps.title: Translate the word problem into an equation
steps.content: Let $x$ be the positive number. Then the number 13 less than $x$ is $x-13$. Their product is given as $$ x(x-13) = 378 $$.

steps.title: Find two factors of 378 that differ by 13
steps.content:
Factor pairs of 378:
  • 1 × 378
  • 2 × 189
  • 3 × 126
  • 6 × 63
  • 7 × 54
  • 9 × 42
  • 14 × 27
. The pair 14 and 27 differs by 13 and multiplies to 378. Since $x$ is the larger factor, $$ x = 27 $$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
